title: JTB Venn Diagram - Justified True Belief
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Theory of Knowledge
description: Interactive Venn diagram showing how knowledge emerges from the intersection of belief, truth, and justification in the classical JTB analysis of knowledge.

educational.topic: Justified True Belief (JTB) Analysis of Knowledge
learningObjectives: Explain how knowledge emerges from the intersection of belief, truth, and justification
prerequisites: Familiarity with the distinction between opinion and knowledge

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive exploration with hover tooltips and quiz mode
keyQuestions: Why is a lucky guess not considered knowledge?
What happens when you have justification and truth but not belief?
Can you think of a real-world example where all three conditions are met?
commonMisconceptions: Confusing belief with truth — students may assume that believing something makes it true
Thinking justification alone is sufficient for knowledge
Overlooking that the JTB model has known limitations (Gettier cases)
assessmentOpportunities: Quiz mode tests students' ability to match scenarios to the correct epistemic region
Ask students to create their own examples for each region
